articles.title: KPMG integrates Claude across its core business and workforce of more than 276,000 in strategic alliance
articles.description: KPMG announced a global strategic alliance with Anthropic, embedding Claude inside Digital Gateway — the software KPMG's people and clients use for tax and legal work. All 276,000+ KPMG employees worldwide will gain access to Claude, and the two companies will jointly build new Claude-powered products for private equity portfolio companies.

articles.title: Anthropic acquires Stainless
articles.description: Anthropic acquired Stainless, a developer-tools startup specializing in SDK generation and MCP server tooling, for more than $300 million. Stainless had been backed by Sequoia Capital and Andreessen Horowitz, and its tooling was already used by OpenAI, Google, and Cloudflare. The acquisition deepens Anthropic's investment in the developer ecosystem around Claude.

articles.title: OpenAI Commits $234 Million for New AI Lab in Singapore
articles.description: OpenAI announced a $234 million commitment to establish its first applied AI lab outside the United States, in partnership with the Singaporean government. The lab will focus on applied research and local AI development. The move is part of OpenAI's broader international expansion strategy.

articles.title: Five takeaways from Nvidia's earnings, and what they mean for the AI industry
articles.description: Nvidia reported record Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with data center revenue surging 92% to $75.2 billion. CEO Jensen Huang described AI demand as "parabolic" as Blackwell systems ramped across hyperscalers and cloud customers. The company also approved an $80 billion share buyback and raised its quarterly dividend 25x, signaling strong confidence in sustained AI infrastructure spending.

articles.title: Intuit to lay off over 3,000 employees to refocus on AI
articles.description: Intuit announced it will cut more than 3,000 employees — roughly 17% of its workforce — as the company redirects resources toward AI integration across its financial software products. The layoffs follow a broader industry trend of established software companies restructuring headcount to fund AI-first product strategies.

articles.title: Trump delays AI security executive order: 'I don't want to get in the way of that leading'
articles.description: President Trump has delayed signing an executive order that would have required AI developers to give the US government early access to models for security evaluation before release. Trump cited a desire not to impede US AI leadership as his reason for the delay. The postponement comes despite Google, Microsoft, xAI, OpenAI, and Anthropic having already agreed to participate in the pre-release review program.

articles.title: I/O 2026: Welcome to the agentic Gemini era
articles.description: At Google I/O 2026, Sundar Pichai unveiled the company's shift to an agent-first strategy, anchored by new models Gemini 3.5 Flash and Gemini Omni and a new personal AI agent called Gemini Spark. Gemini 3.5 Flash is now the default model powering AI Mode in Google Search globally, offering frontier-level reasoning at Flash speeds. Google also launched a $100/month AI Ultra subscription tier and unified its developer platform under Antigravity, its agentic development framework.

articles.title: Anthropic says it's about to have its first profitable quarter
articles.description: Anthropic has informed investors that it expects to generate approximately $10.9 billion in revenue during Q2 2026, more than double the $4.8 billion it recorded in Q1, and will deliver its first-ever operating profit. The company's annualized run-rate revenue has now surpassed $30 billion, up from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. The milestone comes as Anthropic continues rapid commercial expansion and is reportedly eyeing a 2026 IPO.

articles.title: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25B per month for compute
articles.description: Anthropic has struck a multi-year agreement with SpaceX to exclusively use all compute capacity at xAI's Colossus 1 data center in Memphis, Tennessee, paying $1.25 billion per month through May 2029. The deal reflects Anthropic's aggressive push to secure GPU capacity at scale as demand for Claude continues to surge. It also represents an unusual cross-competitor infrastructure arrangement in the AI industry.

articles.title: Anthropic, Microsoft in talks for AI chip deal after $5 billion investment
articles.description: Microsoft and Anthropic are in negotiations for Microsoft to supply its custom Maia 200 AI chips to Anthropic, following Microsoft's recent $5 billion investment in the company. The deal would diversify Anthropic's compute supply away from Nvidia GPUs and further deepen the two companies' commercial relationship. It also signals Microsoft's ambition to establish Maia 200 as a competitive alternative in the AI infrastructure market.

articles.title: Microsoft's GitHub was positioned to win the AI coding race. Outages got in the way
articles.description: GitHub Copilot has lost its lead in the AI coding market to Cursor, Claude Code, and Google Gemini Code Assist, with a March survey of 636 software professionals finding Copilot less widely used than its rivals. A series of service outages has compounded GitHub's challenges at a critical time for developer adoption. Cursor, which recently secured a deal giving SpaceX acquisition rights at a $60 billion valuation, overtook Copilot in market share roughly a year ago and has maintained the lead.

articles.title: The AI Trainers Charging $25,000 a Day to Push Wall Street's Agentic Shift
articles.description: A new class of highly specialized AI consultants is commanding fees of up to $25,000 a day to help Wall Street banks navigate the shift toward agentic AI workflows. These trainers advise financial institutions on identifying gaps in their current AI strategies and preparing teams for autonomous AI systems. The trend reflects how enterprise AI adoption in finance has moved beyond pilot programs into firm-wide operational rewiring.

articles.title: Project Glasswing: An initial update
articles.description: Anthropic published a first progress update on Project Glasswing, its initiative to harden critical software using Claude Mythos Preview. Partners including AWS, Apple, Cisco, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Microsoft, and NVIDIA have collectively identified more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities, with Mythos flagging 23,019 potential flaws in open-source projects alone. Progress on remediation is now the binding constraint: the pace of vulnerability discovery has outrun the speed at which fixes can be verified, disclosed, and patched.

articles.title: Anthropic's restricted Claude Mythos model may be coming to Claude Code
articles.description: Bleeping Computer reports that Anthropic intends to make Mythos-class models available through a general release in the near future, which would include access via Claude Code. Currently restricted to Project Glasswing partners, Mythos Preview has identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities—including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug and a 16-year-old FFmpeg flaw missed by prior tooling. A Claude Security tool built on Mythos is already in public beta for Claude Enterprise customers.

articles.title: Google unveils AI model Gemini 3.5 and AI agent Gemini Spark
articles.description: At Google I/O 2026, Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, a model that rivals large flagships in reasoning while maintaining Flash-series speed and outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agentic benchmarks. Google also announced Gemini Spark, a general-purpose AI agent in the Gemini app that reasons across connected applications and acts on users' behalf, available first to Google AI Ultra subscribers. A third model, Gemini Omni, was unveiled as a world model for multimodal creation and editing from any input type.

articles.title: 100 things we announced at Google I/O 2026
articles.description: Google's official I/O 2026 roundup covers over 100 product and model announcements spanning Android XR intelligent eyewear with Gemini launching this fall, Gemini 3.5 Flash as the new default in Search AI Mode globally, and expanded Project Astra capabilities across the Android ecosystem. The keynote also featured voice-driven productivity upgrades in Gmail, Docs, and Keep, and a new design tool called Google Pics. The breadth of announcements signals Google's strategy to position Gemini as the connective layer across all consumer and developer products.

articles.title: What's New in Cursor — Latest Updates & Release Notes
articles.description: Cursor 3.4 introduced agent development environment tools that automate engineering tasks end-to-end, including repository cloning, dependency installation, and access to internal credentials and build systems. The release also brought Cursor into Microsoft Teams, allowing teams to mention @Cursor in any channel to delegate coding tasks to a cloud agent that automatically selects the right model and repository. Cursor has grown to over 4 million developers, backed by a $400 million funding round at a $10 billion valuation.

articles.title: OpenAI revenue chief Dresser says enterprise AI adoption is 'at a tipping point'
articles.description: OpenAI's revenue chief told CNBC that enterprise AI adoption has reached a tipping point, pointing to approximately $5.7 billion in first-quarter 2026 revenue driven by Codex, expanded enterprise contracts, and early advertising experiments on ChatGPT. The company is seeing accelerating demand across financial services, air carriers, and manufacturers deploying agentic workflows for tasks such as flight rebooking, meeting action capture, and new product development. The revenue trajectory marks a sharp acceleration from prior quarters and reflects broad corporate uptake beyond early adopters.

articles.title: Seoul becomes Anthropic's third office in Asia-Pacific as company appoints KiYoung Choi as Representative Director of Korea
articles.description: Anthropic opened its Seoul office, marking its third presence in Asia-Pacific and signaling continued expansion in the region. KiYoung Choi was appointed as Representative Director to lead the Korea operation. The move reflects growing enterprise demand for Claude across Korean government, financial services, and technology sectors.

articles.title: Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action
articles.description: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O 2026, positioning it as its strongest coding model to date and a cornerstone for agentic workflows. The model offers frontier-level intelligence with a 1M-token context window, full multimodal input, and benchmarks that surpass Gemini 3.1 Pro, at 4× the speed of comparable frontier models. Pricing starts at $1.50/$9 per million tokens and the model is generally available across Google AI Studio, Vertex AI, and the Gemini app.

articles.title: Anthropic forms $200 million partnership with the Gates Foundation
articles.description: Anthropic and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation announced a four-year, $200 million partnership combining funding, Claude usage credits, and technical support for programs in global health, life sciences, education, and economic mobility. The deal represents one of the largest philanthropic AI commitments to date, and aims to direct frontier AI capabilities toward underserved populations and development goals. Claude will be integrated into Gates Foundation-backed initiatives across multiple continents.

articles.title: PwC is deploying Claude to build technology, execute deals, and reinvent enterprise functions for clients
articles.description: PwC expanded its strategic alliance with Anthropic, deepening its use of Claude across technology build, M&A execution, and enterprise transformation engagements for clients. The expanded agreement covers internal productivity use cases as well as client-facing Claude-powered products. PwC joins a growing list of Big Four and global consulting firms formalizing deep Claude integrations.

articles.title: GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing
articles.description: GitHub announced that all Copilot plans will transition to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026, replacing flat premium request quotas with AI Credits priced at $0.01 each. Copilot Pro+ ($39/mo) and Copilot Business ($19/user/mo) will include a monthly credit allotment equal to the plan price, with code completions remaining unlimited. The shift drew developer pushback over concerns that equal spending will now cover less AI usage than before.

articles.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
articles.description: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8, claiming the top spot for computer-use and browser-agent tasks with an 84% score on Online-Mind2Web — a meaningful jump over Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5. The release adds dynamic workflows in Claude Code for large-scale parallel tasks, user-controlled effort levels on claude.ai, and a fast mode running at 2.5× speed at the same price of $5/$25 per million input/output tokens.

articles.title: Meta Unveils Subscriptions for AI While OpenAI, xAI Target Meta's Ad Business
articles.description: Meta announced a paid subscription tier for its consumer AI chatbot, marking a significant monetization shift beyond its core advertising model. The same Bloomberg report notes that OpenAI and xAI are moving aggressively to capture a share of Meta's advertising business, opening a multi-front competitive battle between the major AI players.

articles.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn't be the first partner to feel burned
articles.description: OpenAI is reportedly exploring legal action against Apple over a ChatGPT integration inside Apple Intelligence that failed to deliver the subscriber growth and prominence the company expected. The dispute follows Apple's decision to anchor Apple Intelligence primarily around Google's Gemini models, with Apple paying Google roughly $1 billion per year for the arrangement.

articles.title: Google brings agentic AI and vibe-coded widgets to Android
articles.description: Google unveiled Gemini Intelligence-branded features at its Android Show: I/O Edition event, enabling on-device AI agents to complete tasks across apps, browse the web, and fill out forms autonomously. Users can also vibe-code custom Android home screen widgets using natural language, giving Gemini a deeper integration into the Android UI than any previous release.

articles.title: Anthropic upgrades Claude with new Opus 4.8 model, here's what's new
articles.description: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8, bringing stronger benchmark performance, improved reasoning, and enhanced collaboration capabilities over its predecessor. The launch introduces effort control on claude.ai—letting users dial in how hard Claude works on a task—and dynamic workflows in Claude Code for tackling large-scale engineering problems. API access is available immediately at the same price points as Opus 4.7.

articles.title: Introducing a new design for Microsoft 365 Copilot
articles.description: Microsoft redesigned Microsoft 365 Copilot, transforming its static prompt bar into a task-aware workspace that adapts to the user's current activity across Word, Excel, Teams, and Outlook. The new interface positions Copilot as a more proactive assistant rather than a passive question-answering box, with tighter integration into document and meeting workflows. The redesign is rolling out to commercial Microsoft 365 subscribers immediately.

articles.title: What's new in Copilot Studio: new and improved computer-using agents, a new Workflows experience, and real-time voice
articles.description: Microsoft's May 2026 Copilot Studio update makes computer-using agents generally available, adds a redesigned Workflows experience for enterprise orchestration, and introduces real-time voice interactions for customer-facing agents. The release also includes new governance capabilities—multi-agent management, audit logs, and scoped secrets—marking a shift from low-code chatbot builder to a production-grade enterprise agent platform. The update is available to Copilot Studio customers now.

articles.title: The next phase of enterprise AI
articles.description: OpenAI launched a new $4 billion enterprise deployment company in partnership with 19 global investment firms, consultancies, and system integrators to accelerate large-scale AI adoption. The initiative moves OpenAI beyond its API-first model toward hands-on engagement: helping enterprises identify high-impact use cases and redesign critical workflows around AI. OpenAI framed the move as a necessary step to translate individual AI productivity gains into organization-wide outcomes.

articles.title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age
articles.description: OpenAI published a detailed overview of its compute infrastructure strategy, outlining how it plans to scale training and inference capacity to support increasingly capable AI systems. The post identifies compute, energy, and software stack as the three critical bottlenecks and details partnerships and proprietary investments being made at each layer. OpenAI frames this build-out as foundational to what it calls the "Intelligence Age."

articles.title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation
articles.description: Anthropic closed a $65 billion Series H round led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, pushing its post-money valuation to $965 billion and eclipsing rival OpenAI for the first time. The company reports a $47 billion annual revenue run rate and $10 billion in revenue last year. Proceeds will fund safety and interpretability research, expanded compute capacity, and product and partnership scaling.

articles.title: Scaling Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber
articles.description: OpenAI introduced GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber, its most capable models for cybersecurity tasks, available through its Trusted Access for Cyber program. The models are designed for permissive use by vetted security professionals, with Advanced Account Security becoming mandatory for program members starting June 1, 2026. The release marks OpenAI's deepest push yet into the professional cybersecurity market.

articles.title: OpenAI launches the OpenAI Deployment Company to help businesses build around intelligence
articles.description: OpenAI announced the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new entity that will partner directly with enterprises to design and execute AI deployments around its models. The move is a significant expansion beyond API access, positioning OpenAI as a hands-on implementation partner alongside its existing consulting push. It follows OpenAI's earlier hire of enterprise-focused consultants and signals a shift toward capturing more of the enterprise services market.

articles.title: Teaching Claude why
articles.description: Anthropic published a research post detailing its approach to teaching Claude not just what behaviors are expected but the underlying reasoning and values behind them, aiming to build models that can generalize to novel situations. The work is part of Anthropic's broader interpretability and alignment research agenda, seeking to make Claude's decision-making more robust and principled rather than pattern-matched. The post offers transparency into how Anthropic's safety training philosophy translates into model development practice.

articles.title: How long is Anthropic's lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary
articles.description: TechCrunch reported that Anthropic's multi-billion-dollar compute agreement with SpaceX for access to the Colossus 1 data center contains a 180-day term with a 90-day mutual cancellation clause, raising questions about the deal's long-term stability despite its headline-grabbing $45 billion price tag. Differing interpretations of the contract terms led to public confusion about whether Anthropic has secured multi-year compute or a shorter-term arrangement. The piece highlights how compute agreements have become a key battleground for AI labs seeking to signal strength to investors and customers.

articles.title: Gen-Z Gamer's 3D-Model Startup Vast Becomes China's Latest AI Unicorn
articles.description: Vast, a Chinese 3D-modeling startup founded by a 29-year-old gaming entrepreneur, raised nearly $200 million in a new funding round, reaching a $1 billion valuation and becoming China's latest AI unicorn. The company applies AI to power its 3D content creation platform, serving the gaming and entertainment industries. The milestone underscores continued investor appetite for applied AI startups in China despite the broader macroeconomic environment.

articles.title: Expanding Project Glasswing
articles.description: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing to approximately 150 new organizations across more than 15 countries, deploying Claude Mythos to find critical software vulnerabilities in power, water, healthcare, and communications infrastructure. Since the initiative launched, partners have identified over 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities using Claude Mythos Preview, including zero-days across all major operating systems and browsers. Major partners include Apple, Nvidia, Microsoft, CrowdStrike, and Palo Alto Networks.

articles.title: Anthropic files to go public
articles.description: Anthropic has confidentially filed draft IPO paperwork with the SEC, joining a wave of major AI companies preparing to list on public markets. The filing comes as the company's revenue run rate has reached $47 billion, up from $10 billion annually in 2025, following a funding round that valued it at $965 billion — topping rival OpenAI. Anthropic aims to list as early as fall 2026, potentially ahead of OpenAI in the race to Wall Street.

articles.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
articles.description: OpenAI's frontier models including GPT-5.5, Codex, and Managed Agents are now generally available on Amazon Web Services, allowing enterprises to build AI applications within AWS-native security and governance controls. Teams can power Codex directly through Amazon Bedrock, enabling organizations with existing AWS commitments to access OpenAI's coding agent suite without additional procurement friction. The integration also exposes OpenAI's Managed Agents SDK for orchestrating multi-step agentic workflows in cloud-native environments.

articles.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
articles.description: Microsoft has open-sourced the Agent Control Specification (ACS), a new standard giving developers a consistent and granular way to define what AI agents are permitted to do at runtime. The ACS ships as an SDK with plug-ins for major agent frameworks including LangChain, OpenAI Agents SDK, Anthropic Agents SDK, AutoGen, CrewAI, Semantic Kernel, and MCP tools. The specification addresses growing enterprise concerns around auditability, safety boundaries, and policy enforcement in production agentic deployments.

articles.title: Copilot SDK is now generally available
articles.description: GitHub has released the Copilot SDK as generally available, allowing developers to embed Copilot's agentic engine directly into their own applications, services, and developer tools via a stable, production-ready API. Third-party platforms can now integrate GitHub Copilot's code generation and agentic capabilities natively, extending the reach of Copilot well beyond GitHub's own interfaces. The GA release marks a strategic shift for GitHub, positioning Copilot as an embeddable platform rather than a standalone product.

articles.title: Google Gemini AI Search Shift Is Freaking Out the Online Ad World
articles.description: Bloomberg examines the growing alarm among digital publishers and online advertisers as Google's Gemini-powered AI Mode, now used by over one billion monthly users, increasingly answers queries without directing traffic to external websites. AI Mode — upgraded to Gemini 3.5 Flash as its global default — is accelerating the collapse of the traditional web-search referral model that underpins online advertising. Analysts warn that Google's evolution from a link aggregator to an AI answer engine raises fundamental questions about the future economics of the open web.

articles.title: Introducing OpenAI for Government
articles.description: OpenAI launched OpenAI for Government, a dedicated product offering providing U.S. and international government agencies with access to its AI models under specialized terms and compliance-oriented features. The program is designed to help public-sector organizations modernize operations with enterprise-grade AI while meeting the unique data governance and security requirements of government deployments. The initiative also includes a commitment to advancing democratic governance of frontier AI.

articles.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
articles.description: Microsoft unveiled ASSERT (Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing), a tool that lets developers create AI agent behavior tests using plain-text descriptions instead of hand-coded test cases. ASSERT automatically generates scored test scenarios from natural language specifications, reducing the manual effort required to validate how AI agents behave in production. The tool is designed to complement ACS as part of a broader Microsoft framework for governing and evaluating AI agent deployments.

articles.title: OpenAI deprecates Evals platform, Agent Builder, and reusable prompts
articles.description: OpenAI notified developers on June 3 that it is deprecating the Evals platform, Agent Builder, and reusable prompt objects from its dashboard and API. Older GPT Image models were also flagged for removal from the API on December 1, 2026. The deprecations reflect ongoing consolidation of OpenAI's developer tooling as the company streamlines its product surface.

articles.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
articles.description: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing — its joint industry initiative to identify and fix critical software vulnerabilities using AI — to roughly 150 new organizations across more than 15 countries. Claude Mythos, Anthropic's most capable model yet, powers the effort and has demonstrated the ability to surface thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities over multi-week engagements. The scale-up represents a significant push into AI-driven cybersecurity for critical infrastructure globally.

articles.title: Codex for every role, tool, and workflow
articles.description: OpenAI launched six role-specific plugins for Codex that bundle relevant apps, skills, and instructions for data analytics, creative production, and other professional workflows — spanning 62 popular apps and 110 skills. The announcement accompanies a milestone of over 5 million weekly Codex users, with non-developers now comprising 20% of the user base and growing more than 3x as fast as developers. New features include in-context annotations and a preview for building shareable interactive websites directly from Codex.

articles.title: Microsoft Build 2026 live blog
articles.description: Microsoft Build 2026 (June 2–3, San Francisco) centered on agentic AI, with the debut of seven new MAI models including MAI-Thinking-1, a 35-billion-parameter reasoning model with a 256K context window built without distillation and entering private preview on Azure Foundry. Microsoft introduced Autopilots — long-running autonomous agents, starting with Scout — and the Microsoft IQ context layer that feeds agents real-time workplace, data, and web knowledge. GitHub Copilot also received updates aimed at developers and non-developer roles alike.

articles.title: Infosys, TCS and Wipro scale Microsoft 365 Copilot to over 300,000 employees
articles.description: Microsoft announced that Infosys, TCS, and Wipro have each individually scaled Microsoft 365 Copilot deployments past 100,000 employees, collectively surpassing 300,000 seats across the three firms in under six months. The milestone illustrates the pace of enterprise AI adoption among large IT services organizations, with Microsoft noting it will make 365 Business Standard and Premium with Copilot permanent SKUs starting July 1, 2026.

articles.title: Musk's xAI Pauses Hiring for Specialists to Train Grok Chatbot
articles.description: Elon Musk's xAI has paused hiring for the domain specialists who train Grok on specialized skills, signaling a pivot in how the company builds model capabilities. The pause follows SpaceX's February 2026 absorption of xAI, after which more than 50 researchers and engineers — including the head of the pre-training team — departed. xAI simultaneously launched Grok Connectors, enabling deep integrations with SharePoint, Google Workspace, GitHub, and other enterprise tools, suggesting a near-term focus on product utility over model training breadth.

articles.title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
articles.description: Anthropic filed a confidential draft Form S-1 registration statement with the SEC for a proposed initial public offering, potentially leapfrogging rival OpenAI in the race to Wall Street. The filing follows a $65 billion funding round at a near-$1 trillion valuation and comes as Anthropic reportedly hit $10.9 billion in revenue during Q2 2026. The company has not yet disclosed the IPO timeline or proposed share price.

articles.title: Anthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network
articles.description: Anthropic launched the Services Track and Partner Hub of the Claude Partner Network, backed by a $100 million investment to build an ecosystem of deployment and integration partners. The program creates a formal pathway for consulting and services firms to bring Claude capabilities to enterprise customers. The initiative is part of Anthropic's broader push to accelerate enterprise AI adoption ahead of its planned IPO.

articles.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri's highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
articles.description: Apple's WWDC 2026 opens today with major AI announcements centered on a sweeping Siri overhaul that leverages Google's Gemini technology to transform the assistant into a full conversational AI competing with ChatGPT and Claude. Apple is expected to announce iOS 27, macOS 27, and updates across all platforms featuring new Apple Intelligence capabilities with a strong emphasis on on-device inference as a privacy-preserving alternative to cloud processing. Image Playground, memory features, and multi-step task handling across apps are also expected to debut.

articles.title: OpenAI Readies 'Superapp' Pivot Ahead of Planned IPO, FT Reports
articles.description: OpenAI is planning a major platform overhaul to transform ChatGPT from a question-and-answer chatbot into a comprehensive "superapp" ahead of its anticipated IPO, with changes set to roll out in the coming weeks. The pivot is aimed at deepening user engagement and expanding ChatGPT's utility beyond standalone Q&A to cover a broader range of daily tasks and services. The move mirrors strategies seen in Asian super-apps and is intended to strengthen OpenAI's consumer revenue base alongside its growing enterprise business.
